claim_boundary: Read+review reconciliation of the electrical block-netlist + BOM against the mechanical CAD assembly-manifest (258 parts) and cad-connection-coverage.json (32 modeled CAD connection records, 32/32 passing local marker checks). It is not routed copper, not ERC/DRC, not a fabricated flex drawing, not a built unit. ACCOUNTED means the high-level interconnect family appears in the electrical plan, has a BOM line, and has a mechanical representation (part, flex/cable marker, RF-feed marker, or modeled keepout/connector); it does NOT mean release-ready.

register.interconnect: Display MIPI-DSI + touch I2C + backlight FPC (40-pin)
register.mate: J_DISPLAY_TOUCH (block-netlist) - 10 DSI HS nets + DISP_BL_EN/PWM + TOUCH_I2C + DISP_AVDD_5V5/AVEE_N5V5
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom display_touch (40pin display FPC); bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect 'Display FPC'
register.gap: Connector body modeled but pin count not parameterized in CAD; touch controller MPN still unspecified (display review blocker). Bend path modeled (bend_keepout, r=1.0mm).

register.interconnect: Rear camera MIPI-CSI FPC + connector (4-lane, 24-30 pin)
register.cad_part: rear_camera_module plus rear_camera_fpc_tail and rear camera window/baffle stack
register.mate: J_CAM0 (block-netlist) - CAM0 4 CSI lanes + I2C + AF I2C + reset/pwdn
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom rear_camera (24-30pin camera FPC conn); bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect 'camera FPC x2'
register.gap: Electrical mate, BOM line, module body, and visible rear_camera_fpc_tail are modeled. Still non-release until supplier FPC drawing, connector pin order, bend stack, and production STEP are accepted.

register.cad_part: front_camera_module plus front_camera_fpc_tail under the glass stack
register.mate: J_CAM1 (block-netlist) - CAM1 2 CSI lanes + I2C + reset
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom front_camera (small camera FPC conn); bom-unit-cost 'camera FPC x2'
register.gap: Electrical mate, BOM line, module body, and visible front_camera_fpc_tail are modeled. Supplier FPC drawing, connector pin order, optical stack tolerance, and production STEP remain release blockers.

register.interconnect: Battery FPC/wires to charger + NTC thermistor + PCM
register.mate: J_BATTERY (block-netlist) VBAT/GND + BAT_NTC + BAT_ID -> U_CHARGER CHG_TS
register.bom_line: bom-unit-cost battery_fpc ('connector/wire harness integrated in pack price'); battery_protection_pcm ('bundled with pack')
register.gap: Electrical + BOM present (4-pin pack: VBAT/GND/NTC/ID) and a battery_connector_lead_flex is modeled from the pouch toward the PCB. Exact connector/solder-tab MPN, strain relief, pack vendor drawing, and PCM/NTC pinout signoff remain release blockers.

register.type: side-key flex OR board-edge SMT switch
register.cad_part: power_button_cap, volume_button_cap, gaskets, labyrinth rails, and side_key_flex_tail
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom side_buttons qty3 XKB TS-1187A-B-A-B; bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect 'side-button FPC'
register.gap: Caps, seals, rails, and side_key_flex_tail are modeled. The switch MPN/actuator stack, exact FPC stackup, and force-travel first-article evidence remain release blockers.

register.bom_line: preliminary-bom usb_c_receptacle GCT USB4105; bom-unit-cost line present
register.gap: Receptacle, reinforcement saddle, aperture, drip-lip, drain shelf, 4 gaskets all modeled. USB-C is on the bottom island so its USB2 pair must traverse the split flex (see top-bottom interconnect); USB2 SI across flex is an open electrical blocker but the connector itself is fully accounted.

register.type: hybrid power/signal FPC bridge (Hirose BM28 class) OR board-to-board
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom top_bottom_interconnect Hirose BM28; bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect
register.gap: Both connector bodies + flex routing are modeled in CAD, the PCB is now represented as top/bottom rigid islands, and the 49-contact allocation covers 25 active nets, 18 GND, and 6 spare. Exact mating MPN, circuit count, flex stackup, USB2 SI, current rise, and production schematic/footprint signoff remain release blockers.

register.bom_line: bom-unit-cost antennas 'Cellular main + diversity FPC ... laser-cut copper-on-PI'
register.gap: BOM names the antenna set and CAD includes a visible cellular main RF feed development envelope. Spring/contact/coax choice, antenna vendor drawing, RF matching, VNA/SAR evidence, and production STEP remain release blockers.
Diversity feed now has a distinct visible development envelope. Antenna element geometry, exact feed/contact technology, RF matching, VNA/SAR evidence, and supplier drawings remain release blockers.

Both Wi-Fi/BT RF ports have visible development feed envelopes plus the side antenna keepout. Exact antenna element, coexistence tuning, feed/contact technology, and RF validation remain release blockers.

register.bom_line: not separately lined; folded into cellular antenna set / cellular module
register.gap: CELL_GNSS_RF now has a visible development feed envelope. The antenna element and BOM treatment are still shared/folded into the cellular antenna set, so supplier antenna drawings, matching, GNSS sensitivity, VNA/SAR, and production STEP remain release blockers.

register.interconnect: Antenna aperture tuner (Qorvo QPC1252Q) RFFE + antenna connection
register.type: SMT IC + RFFE control bus + RF series element
register.mate: RFFE bus from U_CELL (RG255C modem); placement in cellular_bottom antenna feed region per params
register.gap: Tuner IC modeled in CAD, BOM-lined, placed in the cellular_bottom feed region. RFFE control is modem-programmed (params). Tuner-to-antenna-element RF link now has development feed-envelope context, but production antenna/contact drawings and RF measurements remain blocked.

register.type: spring contacts or solder pads to bottom-island audio amp
register.cad_part: bottom_speaker_module + acoustic_chamber + bottom_speaker_lead_pair CAD connection marker
register.gap: Module on bottom island near the amp and bottom_speaker_lead_pair is represented in cad-connection-coverage.json for local collision review. Exact spring/solder-tab method, contact metallurgy, pad geometry, and vendor drawing still need release freeze.

Receiver, gasket, acoustic path, and earpiece_receiver_lead_flex marker are modeled for local CAD review. Exact receiver contact method, codec routing, vendor drawing, SPL/leak measurements, and first-article evidence remain release blockers.

register.cad_part: bottom_mic, top_mic, ports/meshes, bottom_microphone_flex_leads, and top_microphone_flex_tail
register.gap: Bottom and top microphones, acoustic ports/meshes, bottom microphone leads, and top_microphone_flex_tail are modeled. Exact MEMS MPN, acoustic mesh stack, PDM routing validation, SNR/leak measurements, and first-article evidence remain release blockers.

register.bom_line: bundled in audio subsystem; HAPTIC_OUT crosses split flex (haptic_test net)
register.gap: LRA body and haptic_flex_tail are modeled, and HAPTIC_OUT is allocated on the split flex. Driver MPN/location, adhesive/preload, vendor drawing, and force/vibration first-article evidence remain release blockers.

register.interconnect: SIM / eSIM (tray contacts or eSIM solder)
register.type: nano-SIM tray w/ contacts + eSIM solder
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom sim_tray; bom-unit-cost sim_tray (nano-SIM tray + gasket)
register.gap: Tray outline, keepout, and SIM/eSIM signal-flex marker are modeled. Exact tray/contact MPN, eSIM solder site, gasket, modem pinout freeze, and side-key/SIM stack tolerance remain release blockers.

register.type: drive lines from flash driver IC
register.mate: I2C flash driver AW36515 (block-netlist NFC/sensor I2C class; driver not its own block)
register.gap: LED modeled, window/baffle/septum modeled, LED + driver IC both BOM-lined. Driver is not a distinct block in block-netlist (rides the I2C bus) but is electrically implied and BOM-present. 1.0x1.0 seat now matches a 1.0x1.0 emitter (optical review fix applied).

register.cad_part: compute_som_sodimm_connector + compute_som_daughterboard_keepout modeled as PATH A carrier connector and swept module keepout
register.mate: U_SOC_LPDDR_UFS SODIMM_260P_0P5_COMPUTE_SOM_DEV footprint in routed-development KiCad, with public Core-3566JD4 pinout pads assigned on key phone nets
register.bom_line: bom-unit-cost PATH A = Firefly Core-3566JD4 RK3566 SoM, 260-pin SODIMM 0.5mm (default cost path)
register.gap: SODIMM connector and daughterboard swept keepout are now CAD-visible and tied to the KiCad SoM footprint. This is local collision-review coverage only: exact socket MPN, supplier STEP, module stack height, insertion clearance, shielding, and measured EVT fit remain release blockers.

som_vs_bare_soc.mechanical_assumes: Generated CAD now carries the PATH A SoM interface as compute_som_sodimm_connector plus compute_som_daughterboard_keepout, while chip-down package markers remain as PATH B cost-down context.
som_vs_bare_soc.bom_ships: PATH A turnkey RK3566 SoM (Firefly Core-3566JD4), 260-pin gold-finger SODIMM 0.5mm pitch daughterboard; lines for discrete LPDDR/eMMC/PMIC are zeroed/folded-into-SoM.
som_vs_bare_soc.physical_fit: The SoM stack is CAD-visible but not release fit approved. A 260-pin SODIMM module + edge socket can add a stacked daughterboard (~1.0mm board + components + socket standoff, ~3-4mm Z) near the screen/battery stack, so exact supplier STEP and measured stack fit are still mandatory.
som_vs_bare_soc.recommended_resolution: Keep PATH A as the public-data build path only with explicit SODIMM connector/keepout CAD coverage. Do not claim production enclosure fit until socket MPN, module STEP, shield strategy, insertion clearance, and EVT measurements replace the swept keepout.

split_board_geometry.cad_models: TWO board islands inside the main_pcb composite STEP: top island 64x29mm at board y=0 and bottom island 64x15mm at board y=117, matching layout-utilization Edge.Cuts. The split_interconnect_top_connector, split_interconnect_bottom_connector, and split_interconnect_side_flex bridge the two rigid islands along the +X edge.
split_board_geometry.electrical_plan_selects: TWO rigid islands: board-topology-decision selects top_bottom_rigid_islands_with_flex_or_board_to_board; block-netlist has J_TOP_BOTTOM_FLEX_TOP and _BOTTOM as the two-island bridge (49 contacts).
split_board_geometry.contradiction: resolved_for_concept_cad: the generated CAD no longer uses a single continuous PCB slab for the selected topology; board-step-readiness reports concept_split_island_geometry_matches_kicad=true.
split_board_geometry.recommended_resolution: Keep the split-island CAD representation for local enclosure review. Release still requires replacing the concept composite board with a native routed KiCad STEP containing supplier-approved component models and measured routed-board clearance results.

top_3_missing_partial: ACCOUNTED-NON-RELEASE: RK3566 SoM 260-pin SODIMM connector + daughterboard keepout are now represented in CAD and tied to the KiCad SoM footprint, but exact socket/module STEP and measured stack fit remain blocked.

ACCOUNTED-NON-RELEASE: All requested flex/cable/RF feed marker solids are now represented for local CAD collision review, including earpiece and top microphone leads. Supplier antenna/FPC drawings, controlled-impedance flex stackups, mating connector MPNs, and first-article measurements are still absent.
ACCOUNTED-NON-RELEASE: Development marker solids do not replace routed copper, supplier STEP/B-rep, DRC/ERC/SI/PI/RF signoff, or physical routed-board clearance evidence.
